summary.intent: Implement a lazy tool registry feature that shows the model a single meta-tool (`tool_search`) instead of all tool schemas, reducing token consumption when many MCP servers are connected.
summary.outcome: All four phases fully implemented: ToolRegistry plumbing with activated map, ToolSearchTool with keyword/activation search, config wiring via `registry_mode = "lazy"`, system prompt hints, and docs. All 435 tests pass, clippy clean.

repo: ToolRegistry is in `crates/agents/src/tool_registry.rs` and used by both streaming and non-streaming runner loops in `runner.rs`
Chat integration point for post-filter registry wrapping is in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs` around the `run_with_tools` function, using `persona.config` (type `MoltisConfig`) for config access
Config schema additions require updates in 4 places: `schema.rs` (struct/enum), `validate.rs` (`build_schema_map`), `lib.rs` (re-export), and `template.rs` (docs)
Sub-agents should not inherit activated tools — clone methods produce fresh empty `activated` sets intentionally
The workspace has a pre-existing unrelated build error (missing CSS file) that does not affect agent/chat/config crates

code.finding: Added `ActivatedTools` type alias (`Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<dyn AgentTool>>>>`), `activated` field, `get_arc()`, `has_activated_tools()`, and `entry_to_schema()` helper. Uses std::sync::Mutex (not tokio) since lock is held for microseconds and list_schemas() must remain sync.

Moved `tool_schemas`/`schemas_for_api` computation inside both loop bodies (non-streaming and streaming) so newly activated tools appear on the next iteration. Added 3x max_iterations multiplier when lazy mode detected.

Registry wrapping with `wrap_registry_lazy` inserted after `apply_runtime_tool_filters()` and `install_agent_scoped_memory_tools()`, guarded by `persona.config.tools.registry_mode == ToolRegistryMode::Lazy`

ToolSearchTool scoring: exact name match=100, name contains=50, word overlap=10×count, max 15 results returned as name+description pairs without schemas. Name activation inserts into shared Arc<Mutex<HashMap>> and returns full schema.
Lazy mode detected via presence of `tool_search` in schemas and absence of `memory_search`; adds hint to discover memory tools via tool_search

workflow: Beads/dolt issue tracking was unavailable (server wouldn't start); implementation proceeded without it
Compiling individual crates with `cargo build -p <crate>` is faster for iterative feedback than full workspace builds
Running `cargo clippy` after implementation caught a needless `.into_iter()` call that required a fix before finalizing

friction: Beads/dolt issue tracker server failed to start, making structured task tracking unavailable — had to proceed without it
Pre-existing workspace build error (missing CSS file) created noise when checking compilation status of unrelated crates
Finding the exact chat integration point required multiple grep/read iterations across a large `lib.rs` file to locate `persona.config` vs a bare `config` variable
open_items: PR split strategy from the plan: Phase 1+2 as PR 1 (dead code behind wrap_registry_lazy, no config wiring) and Phase 3+4 as PR 2 — the implementation was committed as a single commit instead, which may need splitting before merge review
Manual QA not yet done: set `registry_mode = "lazy"` in moltis.toml, connect MCP server, verify model uses tool_search to discover and call tools
Token comparison benchmark not yet run: count input tokens per turn in full vs lazy mode with 50+ MCP tools
The `docs/src/SUMMARY.md` update mentioned in the plan was not performed (lazy registry docs were added inline to existing tool-registry.md instead)
